evidence: src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts exposes setErrorPresenter(), which mutates a process-wide notifier bridge through configureErrorNotifier and creates hidden global coupling between feature hooks and UI error presentation.

src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts and src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts call handleError/handleAndRethrow directly, so feature-layer behavior depends on an out-of-band global presenter setup.

src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts exports a live `supabase` legacy facade plus getLegacySupabaseClient(), preserving dual data-access surfaces and keeping migration-era boundaries active.

`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useBatchImageLoading.test.ts` targets `@/shared/hooks/ui-image/useBatchImageLoading`, but test placement stays in a generic hooks test bucket instead of mirroring the module boundary.

`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useBottomOffset.test.ts` imports `../useBottomOffset` while mocking `@/shared/contexts/PanesContext`, showing layout-specific behavior tested from a broad shared test directory.

`src/shared/hooks/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts` contains project/generation orchestration (query invalidation and thumbnail upload) alongside generic infrastructure hooks like `useAsyncOperation` and `useAutoSaveSettings` in the same `shared/hooks` package.
`src/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary.tsx` is an app-shell safety boundary but is housed under reusable `shared/components`, which dilutes package role clarity.
high_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Package roles are mostly understandable, but boundary drift in shared hooks/components and non-mirrored test placement make ownership less predictable for new contributors.

evidence: `useInlineEditState` still exports `toInlineEditCanvasState` and `toInlineEditSidebarState` as pure field-forwarding selectors with no translation/validation, preserving an extra adapter layer (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:537`, `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:558`).

`InlineEditView` still satisfies shared button contracts with inert placeholder values (`handleAddToReferences={async () => {}}`, static false flags), which indicates the shared control interface remains broader than inline-edit needs (`src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx:152`).

`ShotImageManagerContainer` fabricates and casts a synthetic media object to satisfy lightbox expectations (`as GenerationRow`), showing a seam mismatch between caller data and lightbox media contract (`src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx:316`).
abstraction_fitness.issues_preventing_higher_score: Residual interface inflation remains in inline-edit/lightbox integration: adapters and no-op shims still exist where feature-specific contracts should be narrower.

evidence: Segment deletion flow still performs ad-hoc multi-source contract reads (`pair_shot_generation_id` from row, `individual_segment_params`, and top-level params) directly in the callback layer (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:81`).

Task builders already introduced a canonical payload reader pattern (`createTravelPayloadReader`) for the same family, so UI seam logic is now split between canonical and manual fallback styles (`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:299`).

Lightbox handoff from shot manager mutates payload shape at call-site instead of passing explicit seam data, making boundary behavior less predictable (`src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx:316`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/VideoLightbox.tsx:90`).
mid_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Boundary translation is still duplicated across UI and task layers, and some seams still rely on call-site adaptation instead of explicit contracts.

evidence: Three assigned files are pure compatibility pass-throughs (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`, `src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useClickRipple.ts`) that add import indirection without behavior.

Legacy migration markers are runtime-noop only in `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` (`void LEGACY_SUPABASE_ALIAS_REMOVE_BY`, `void LEGACY_SUPABASE_ALIAS_OWNER`), while deprecated aliases remain exported in `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts`.

Unchecked cast-heavy normalization appears in `src/shared/lib/galleryUtils.ts` and `src/shared/lib/preloading/preloader.ts`, where unknown/optional shapes are coerced and consumed via fallback ladders.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` contains high-branch completion/billing logic, but `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.test.ts` only asserts export presence and `orchestrator.gate.test.ts` only covers `evaluateSegmentCompletionGate`.

Critical shared parsers/adapters in this batch (`src/shared/lib/tasks/taskPayloadSnapshot.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/taskParamParsers.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/structureGuidance.ts`, `supabase/functions/_shared/orchestratorReference.ts`) have no direct tests despite being used in `individualTravelSegment`, `complete_task/generation`, and edge lookup paths.

`supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts` includes fallback DB query choreography and HTTP billing trigger behavior, but `supabase/functions/_shared/billing.test.ts` validates only small extraction/filter helpers.

Shot editor controllers are acting as aggregation hubs rather than focused units: `useGenerationController` accepts a very large mixed parameter surface (prompt/motion/model/join/UI settings) and forwards most of it into `useGenerateBatch` (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/controllers/useGenerationController.ts:7-125`, `:155-220`).

`useEditingController` combines structure-video management, audio settings, shot-name editing, join setup, and join execution in one hook and returns a wide flat object (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/controllers/useEditingController.ts:39-156`, `:158-220`).

Lightbox props are repeatedly reshaped across layers (`MediaLightbox` flat props -> bundled groups -> `useLightboxWorkflowProps` -> layout prop objects), increasing indirection and maintenance cost (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx:11-76`, `:78-145`; `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useLightboxWorkflowProps.ts:63-78`, `:90-151`).

Image generation form submission path repeats nearly the same large argument set across `useFormSubmission`, `useSubmissionHandlers`, `usePromptQueueHandlers`, and `useTaskParamsBuilder`, indicating data-clump propagation instead of a stable submission context object (`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/useFormSubmission.ts:21-48`, `:73-108`; `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/useSubmissionHandlers.ts:7-22`, `:44-78`; `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/usePromptQueueHandlers.ts:8-22`; `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/useTaskParamsBuilder.ts:15-33`).

Task detail UI components also perform payload-shape fallback/normalization inline (e.g., checking `parsedParams`, `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`) rather than consuming a normalized view model (`src/shared/components/TaskDetails/CharacterAnimateDetails.tsx:18-27`; `src/shared/components/TaskDetailsModal.tsx:74-102`).

findings.summary: Global error presenter wiring creates hidden cross-module runtime coupling.
evidence: handleError.ts exposes setErrorPresenter() that mutates notifier behavior globally via configureErrorNotifier.
Gallery hooks call handleError/handleAndRethrow without explicit presenter dependency, so UX behavior depends on global bootstrap order and side-channel configuration.
findings.suggestion: Replace global presenter mutation with an explicit error-reporting boundary (provider/context or injected reporter) and keep shared error normalization pure; enforce one app-shell adapter that maps normalized errors to toast UI.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase facade keeps duplicate client entrypoints alive across modules.
evidence: legacySupabaseFacade.ts exports both getLegacySupabaseClient() and a module-level `supabase` facade with broad client capabilities, despite deprecation metadata.
This preserves legacy-vs-canonical access ambiguity and increases blast radius for client contract changes.
findings.suggestion: Constrain legacy access to a migration-only adapter path, block new imports via lint rule, and migrate remaining call sites to getSupabaseClient() until the module-level `supabase` export can be removed.

findings.summary: MediaGalleryItem modules depend on parent MediaGallery types, blurring ownership.
evidence: MediaGalleryItem files import GeneratedImageWithMetadata from ../../MediaGallery/types instead of a neutral domain-level type module.
Multiple hooks/components across gallery and item layers converge on parent-relative type paths, indicating container-first structure rather than stable feature contracts.
findings.suggestion: Extract media gallery domain contracts into a dedicated shared module (e.g., MediaGallery/domain/types) and have both MediaGallery and MediaGalleryItem consume that boundary to remove parent-child type dependency inversion.

findings.summary: Inline-edit still carries low-leverage adapter/shim layers around shared controls
evidence: `toInlineEditCanvasState` and `toInlineEditSidebarState` are straight pass-through mappers over `InlineEditStateResult` with no behavior change (`useInlineEditState.ts:537-575`).

`InlineEditView` passes no-op and static placeholder props (`handleAddToReferences`, `isAddingToReferences`, `addToReferencesSuccess`) solely to satisfy `BottomRightControls` shape (`InlineEditView.tsx:152-160`).
findings.suggestion: Collapse inline-edit selectors into direct state usage at render sites, and split shared button props into variant-specific contracts so inline-edit can omit add-to-reference semantics instead of supplying no-op shims.

findings.summary: Segment callback seam reintroduces manual fallback ladders instead of canonical readers
evidence: Deletion logic manually probes `pair_shot_generation_id` in three different locations (`beforeData.pair_shot_generation_id`, `individual_segment_params`, top-level `params`) and repeats similar lookup for siblings (`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:83-115`).
Task-family code already centralized comparable fallback policy via `buildTaskPayloadSnapshot` + `createTravelPayloadReader` (`individualTravelSegment.ts:298-319`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared travel/segment payload accessor for UI callbacks (mirroring `createTravelPayloadReader` policy) and replace in-callback fallback ladders with one canonical read API.

findings.summary: Shot manager still fabricates/casts lightbox media payloads at the boundary
evidence: `SegmentVideoLightbox` creates a spread object with conditional `parent_generation_id` and force-casts it as `GenerationRow` before passing to `MediaLightbox` (`ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx:316-320`).

Video lightbox flow expects a concrete media contract in media mode (`VideoLightboxPropsWithMedia`), so caller-side casting is masking seam mismatch instead of expressing it in the interface (`VideoLightbox.tsx:90`).
findings.suggestion: Move parent-generation override into an explicit lightbox prop (or a typed adapter at the lightbox boundary) and remove caller-side `GenerationRow` casts so data-shape translation is centralized and type-checked.

findings.summary: Orchestrator completion’s high-risk branches are largely untested.
evidence: `checkOrchestratorCompletion` coordinates sibling lookup fallback, final-step handling, failure marking, completion updates, billing trigger, and reconciliation persistence.
Current tests only assert export presence (`orchestrator.test.ts`) and one pure gate helper (`orchestrator.gate.test.ts`), leaving most production branches unverified.
findings.suggestion: Add orchestrator integration-style unit tests with mocked Supabase/billing covering: run-id lookup fallback, missing/failed/pending segments, final-step wait behavior, mark-failed/mark-complete updates, billing recoverable vs nonrecoverable outcomes, and persistBillingOutcome fallback path.

findings.summary: Shared payload parser/snapshot contracts have no direct tests despite critical usage.
evidence: No test files target these modules directly, but they are imported by task and edge critical paths (`individualTravelSegment`, `complete_task/generation`, orchestrator-reference lookup/billing).
These modules implement permissive coercion/fallback behavior that can silently shape API payload interpretation.
findings.suggestion: Create focused contract tests for malformed/legacy payloads, precedence ordering, and canonical-vs-legacy fallback behavior; include shared fixtures consumed by both app and edge test suites to lock parsing semantics.

findings.summary: Structure-guidance mapping is duplicated across seams and has divergent behavior.
evidence: Both modules build the same outbound `structure_guidance` contract but with independent logic/defaults (`normalizeStructureGuidance` vs `buildStructureGuidance`).
`generateVideoService` hardcodes uni3c `step_window` fallback end to `1.0`, while shared normalization defaults uni3c end percent to `0.1`, creating seam-dependent payload differences.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate to one canonical structure-guidance adapter in shared task libs and route service code through it; keep only one place for defaults and field-mapping rules, then add regression tests for both UI and task-creation callers.

findings.summary: Rate-limit response contract has drift between implementation and verification surface.
evidence: `rateLimitResponse` delegates to `edgeErrorResponse` and emits machine-coded envelope fields (`error`, `errorCode` both code-like values).
The current test asserts `error: 'Too many requests'`, indicating the test contract and runtime envelope are not aligned.
findings.suggestion: Define one explicit edge error envelope contract for rate limiting (code + message + retry metadata), update `rateLimit.test.ts` to assert that contract, and add one consumer-level test to verify handlers return the same envelope shape through `rateLimitResponse`.

findings.summary: Shared billing module lacks tests for fallback query and trigger semantics.
evidence: `billing.ts` includes canonical→legacy query fallback (`lookupCompletedSubTasksForOrchestrator`) and HTTP trigger error classification (`triggerCostCalculation`).
Existing tests only cover `extractOrchestratorRef`, UUID detection, and basic filter string construction.
findings.suggestion: Add tests that simulate canonical-hit, canonical-empty+legacy-hit, canonical/legacy query failures, and triggerCostCalculation HTTP/network failure envelopes; then assert orchestrator consumers handle those outcomes predictably.

findings.summary: Multiple migration shims are pure re-export wrappers with no local behavior.
evidence: `client.ts` only re-exports runtime client accessors.
`cn.ts` only re-exports `cn` from `uiClassNames`.
`useClickRipple.ts` is deprecated and only re-exports from `interaction/useClickRipple`.
findings.suggestion: Run a codemod to migrate imports to canonical modules, add lint/no-restricted-imports rules blocking new shim imports, then delete these wrapper files once import count reaches zero.

findings.summary: Deprecated APIs remain active with metadata-only sunset markers and no enforcement path.
evidence: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` keeps deprecated surface alive and suppresses marker constants through `void` statements, which do not enforce removal.
`handleError.ts` still exports deprecated alias symbols (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) as full active API contracts.
findings.suggestion: Replace metadata-only deprecation with enforceable controls: add runtime/dev warnings on deprecated entrypoints, gate usage in CI, and move deprecated exports behind an explicit legacy module to reduce accidental new adoption.

findings.summary: Utility internals rely on unchecked casts and fallback ladders that hide malformed inputs.
evidence: `galleryUtils.ts` coerces unknown params to `Record<string, unknown>` and arrays to `string[]` via `as`, then indexes into them without validating array element types.
`preloader.ts` casts `PreloadableImage` to `{ id: string }` for tracker APIs (`hasLoadedImage`, `setImageLoadStatus`) even though `id` is optional in `PreloadableImage`.
findings.suggestion: Introduce narrow parser helpers (`isStringArray`, `asTrackableImage`) and use validated branches before indexing/caching; switch preload dedupe to URL-key checks when `id` is absent instead of forced ID casts.

findings.summary: Hook tests are grouped by a generic shared test folder instead of the owning module paths, weakening discoverability and ownership boundaries.
evidence: `useBatchImageLoading.test.ts` imports `@/shared/hooks/ui-image/useBatchImageLoading` even though the test is stored in `shared/hooks/__tests__` rather than a `ui-image`-mirrored location.
`useBottomOffset.test.ts` uses a relative import (`../useBottomOffset`) from the same generic test folder, signaling a catch-all test package pattern rather than module-local test ownership.
The three tests in this folder cover different subdomains (image loading, layout offset behavior, thumbnail generation), indicating directory-based accumulation instead of bounded test placement.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a mirrored test layout (co-located tests or subfolder-mirrored `__tests__`) so each hook’s tests live with its owning domain path, then standardize import style to reflect that boundary.

findings.summary: The `shared` package hosts both cross-cutting primitives and feature/app-specific orchestration, blurring top-level ownership.
evidence: `useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts` performs domain orchestration (generation queueing, upload, query invalidation via `unifiedGenerationQueryKeys`) rather than generic reusable hook behavior.
`useAsyncOperation.ts` and `useAutoSaveSettings.ts` are clearly generic infrastructure hooks but are peers of the feature-coupled thumbnail orchestrator in the same package.
`AppErrorBoundary.tsx` is described and implemented as app-level boundary logic (full-screen recovery UI, reload action), not a generally reusable UI component.
findings.suggestion: Split `shared` into stricter sub-boundaries (for example: `shared/infrastructure` for generic hooks, feature-scoped hook folders for generation-specific workflows, and an `app/shell` location for app-wide boundaries) and move these modules accordingly.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase facade remains a broad parallel client surface, preserving cross-module boundary drift.
evidence: README.md explicitly documents both canonical runtime client access and legacy compatibility imports.
legacySupabaseFacade exports `supabase` with broad parity to the core client (`from`, `rpc`, channels, `auth`, `storage`, `realtime`, `functions`) rather than a minimal migration-only shim.
The facade’s removal condition is documented in comments, but the API remains fully exported and usable.
findings.suggestion: Constrain the legacy facade to a minimal, migration-only API (or internalize it), then drive remaining call sites to `getSupabaseClient()` behind a shrinking compatibility budget with a hard fail threshold before 2026-06-30.

findings.summary: Governance contracts are expressed as docs/comments but not reflected as strong runtime ownership boundaries.
evidence: README positions migration and shim policies as authoritative project contracts.

Both `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` and `handleError.ts` retain deprecated compatibility aliases with owner/removal metadata, but policy enforcement is not encoded at the same boundary where APIs are exposed.
In `legacySupabaseFacade.ts`, deprecation constants are only referenced via `void` expressions, signaling metadata preservation without operational governance behavior.
findings.suggestion: Move governance from passive annotations to active boundary controls: expose deprecated aliases only through explicitly named compat modules, add deadline-aware build/runtime assertions, and align README claims with enforceable import boundaries.

findings.summary: Critical migration/deprecation contracts are not treated as executable test oracles in scoped modules.
evidence: README defines strict compatibility and canonical-surface expectations, but these expectations are not embedded as verifiable contracts in the listed boundary modules.
legacySupabaseFacade relies on comment metadata (`@deprecated`, `@removalTarget`) while continuing to export a production-ready facade.
High-fan-in barrel/alias files (`generationAndShots.ts`, `cn.ts`) provide central contracts without in-module safeguards against accidental API surface expansion or drift.
findings.suggestion: Add contract-focused tests that fail on governance drift: assert allowed exports for `generationAndShots`/`cn`, assert legacy facade surface remains migration-minimal, and add a deadline/budget test tied to the documented 2026-06-30 Supabase shim sunset.

findings.summary: Shot editor controller hooks mix multiple responsibilities and expose oversized interfaces, making coordination changes high-risk.
evidence: `useGenerationController` parameter contract spans prompt composition, motion tuning, join settings, persistence flags, and UI callbacks in one interface (`useGenerationController.ts:7-65`).
The same hook forwards a long near-pass-through configuration to `useGenerateBatch` (`useGenerationController.ts:160-220`), indicating orchestration without clear bounded contexts.

`useEditingController` combines structure-video CRUD, audio state, name editing, join setup, and join execution in one flow (`useEditingController.ts:39-156`) and returns a broad flattened API (`:158-220`).
findings.suggestion: Split controller responsibilities by capability (e.g., `usePromptGenerationController`, `useJoinController`, `useShotMetadataController`) and pass a small typed context object per controller instead of one global params bag; compose controllers in the page boundary only.

findings.summary: Lightbox architecture still performs repeated prop re-bundling across layers, adding indirection without reducing conceptual load.
evidence: `MediaLightbox` defines a very wide flat props interface and then groups it into `navigation`, `shotWorkflow`, `features`, `actions`, `videoProps` via `useBundledLightboxProps` (`MediaLightbox.tsx:11-76`, `:78-145`).
`useLightboxWorkflowProps` redefines grouped sub-interfaces and rebuilds another composed prop object for layout (`useLightboxWorkflowProps.ts:22-78`, `:90-151`).
`useSharedLightboxState` also introduces many concern-specific sub-interfaces and a large composed type, signaling repeated local reshaping rather than one stable boundary contract.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical lightbox controller contract (query + commands) at the media-type boundary and let layout/components consume that directly; avoid successive rebundling layers unless they transform semantics.

findings.summary: Image form submission logic propagates the same large data clumps through multiple hooks, which obscures ownership and increases change coupling.
evidence: `useFormSubmission` destructures a long prop set and forwards overlapping subsets into both `useTaskParamsBuilder` and `useSubmissionHandlers` (`useFormSubmission.ts:21-48`, `:73-108`).
`useSubmissionHandlers` and `usePromptQueueHandlers` define large interfaces with mostly forwarded fields (`useSubmissionHandlers.ts:7-22`; `usePromptQueueHandlers.ts:8-22`).
`useTaskParamsBuilder` reintroduces another similarly large context contract (`useTaskParamsBuilder.ts:15-33`), indicating repeated manual plumbing.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single `SubmissionContext` object (with stable `refs`, policy flags, and prompt state) created once in `useFormSubmission`, then pass that object through subordinate hooks so only behavior-specific deltas are supplied.

findings.summary: Task-detail presentation components embed payload normalization and compatibility fallback logic, mixing data adaptation with UI rendering.
evidence: `CharacterAnimateDetails` manually resolves fields from `parsedParams`, `orchestrator_details`, and `full_orchestrator_payload` in render logic (`CharacterAnimateDetails.tsx:18-27`).
`TaskDetailsModal` derives `inputImages` from multiple payload locations and cleanup rules inline (`TaskDetailsModal.tsx:74-102`) while also managing modal state/fetch lifecycles.
`ImageEditTaskDetails` repeats parse/derive/extract behavior in component scope (`ImageEditTaskDetails.tsx:16-24`).
findings.suggestion: Create a dedicated task-details adapter layer (e.g., `toTaskDetailsViewModel(task)`) that handles payload fallback and normalization once, and keep UI components render-only.

findings.summary: Edge test config is partially duplicated across unit and smoke files, creating avoidable drift risk for shared execution semantics.
evidence: Both configs duplicate `environment`, `globals`, `sequence.concurrent`, `testTimeout`, and `hookTimeout` (`vitest.edge.config.ts:74-84`; `vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts:6-15`).
Only include/exclude and alias differ, but there is no shared base config function or merge utility.
findings.suggestion: Extract shared edge test defaults into a small base config factory (e.g., `createEdgeVitestConfig`) and compose unit/smoke variants by overriding include/exclude/resolve fields only.
